name: K8s Namespace Resource Survey
description: Snapshot a Kubernetes namespace's resource inventory by fanning the trigger out to three parallel openagentic_kubernetes MCP calls (k8s_list_pods, k8s_list_deployments, k8s_list_services). A labeled merge node converges the three native-dict branches keyed by source-node label (pods/deployments/services), a single analyze transform normalizes each branch into a typed array + pre-computes counts + per-section HTML <tr> rows for direct interpolation, the platform LLM produces a 3-4 bullet narrative characterizing the namespace's resource footprint (what runs here, signs of risk, capacity headroom), CoT preamble is stripped via clean_narrative, and the final webhook_response renders an HTML survey report with per-resource tables + narrative. Empty-namespace case (zero pods, deployments, services — the typical state for unused namespaces) is handled: the report renders an explicit 'No resources' banner per section instead of blank tables.

meta.purpose: Inventory a Kubernetes namespace by listing pods, deployments, and services in parallel and producing a single HTML survey with per-section tables plus an LLM narrative on what runs there.
how_it_works: 1. Fans the trigger out to three parallel openagentic_kubernetes MCP calls: k8s_list_pods, k8s_list_deployments, k8s_list_services.
2. A labeled merge converges the three native-dict branches keyed by source-node label.
3. A single analyze transform normalizes each branch into a typed array, pre-computes counts, and renders per-section HTML <tr> rows.
4. The platform LLM writes a 3-4 bullet narrative characterizing the namespace footprint (what runs here, signs of risk, capacity headroom).
5. A CoT-stripper transform removes any LLM preamble.
6. webhook_response renders the HTML survey. Empty-namespace short-circuit: each section gets an explicit 'No resources' banner instead of a blank table.
meta.expected_output: HTML survey report with three resource tables (pods, deployments, services), per-section counts, and a 3-4 bullet LLM characterization of the namespace.
meta.useful_when: Onboarding a new namespace, post-migration verification, capacity planning, or oncall context-building when a namespace you don't own pages.

operations.value: {{ (() => { const branch = input && input.pods; if (!branch || typeof branch !== 'object') return []; if (Array.isArray(branch.pods)) return branch.pods; if (typeof branch.content === 'string') { try { const p = JSON.parse(branch.content).pods; return Array.isArray(p) ? p : []; } catch (e) { return []; } } return []; })() }}

{{ (() => { const branch = input && input.deployments; if (!branch || typeof branch !== 'object') return []; if (Array.isArray(branch.deployments)) return branch.deployments; if (typeof branch.content === 'string') { try { const d = JSON.parse(branch.content).deployments; return Array.isArray(d) ? d : []; } catch (e) { return []; } } return []; })() }}

{{ (() => { const branch = input && input.services; if (!branch || typeof branch !== 'object') return []; if (Array.isArray(branch.services)) return branch.services; if (typeof branch.content === 'string') { try { const s = JSON.parse(branch.content).services; return Array.isArray(s) ? s : []; } catch (e) { return []; } } return []; })() }}

{{ (() => { const tryNs = (b) => { if (!b || typeof b !== 'object') return null; if (typeof b.namespace === 'string' && b.namespace) return b.namespace; if (typeof b.content === 'string') { try { return JSON.parse(b.content).namespace || null; } catch (e) { return null; } } return null; }; return tryNs(input && input.pods) || tryNs(input && input.deployments) || tryNs(input && input.services) || 'unknown'; })() }}
{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.pods_array) ? input.pods_array : []; return a.length; })() }}
{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.deployments_array) ? input.deployments_array : []; return a.length; })() }}
{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.services_array) ? input.services_array : []; return a.length; })() }}

{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.pods_array) ? input.pods_array : []; if (a.length === 0) return 'No pods in this namespace.'; const esc = s => String(s == null ? '' : s).replace(/&/g, '&').replace(//g, '>'); return a.slice(0, 10).map(p => { const name = esc(p && p.name); const status = esc((p && p.status) || ''); const ready = esc((p && p.ready) || ''); const restarts = (p && typeof p.restarts === 'number') ? p.restarts : 0; return `${name}${status}${ready}${restarts}`; }).join(''); })() }}

{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.deployments_array) ? input.deployments_array : []; if (a.length === 0) return 'No deployments in this namespace.'; const esc = s => String(s == null ? '' : s).replace(/&/g, '&').replace(//g, '>'); return a.slice(0, 10).map(d => { const name = esc(d && d.name); const desired = (d && typeof d.replicas === 'number') ? d.replicas : 0; const ready = (d && typeof d.ready_replicas === 'number') ? d.ready_replicas : 0; const available = (d && typeof d.available_replicas === 'number') ? d.available_replicas : 0; return `${name}${ready}/${desired}${available}${esc((d && d.created) || '')}`; }).join(''); })() }}

{{ (() => { const a = Array.isArray(input.services_array) ? input.services_array : []; if (a.length === 0) return 'No services in this namespace.'; const esc = s => String(s == null ? '' : s).replace(/&/g, '&').replace(//g, '>'); return a.slice(0, 10).map(s => { const name = esc(s && s.name); const type = esc((s && s.type) || ''); const clusterIp = esc((s && s.cluster_ip) || ''); const ports = Array.isArray(s && s.ports) ? s.ports.map(p => (p && p.port) ? String(p.port) : '').filter(x => x).join(',') : ''; return `${name}${type}${clusterIp}${esc(ports)}`; }).join(''); })() }}

{{ (() => { const pc = (typeof input.pod_count === 'number') ? input.pod_count : 0; const dc = (typeof input.deployment_count === 'number') ? input.deployment_count : 0; const sc = (typeof input.service_count === 'number') ? input.service_count : 0; if (pc === 0 && dc === 0 && sc === 0) return '

No resources found in this namespace. The namespace is empty (zero pods, deployments, and services).

'; return ''; })() }}

{{ (() => { const pc = (typeof input.pod_count === 'number') ? input.pod_count : 0; const dc = (typeof input.deployment_count === 'number') ? input.deployment_count : 0; const sc = (typeof input.service_count === 'number') ? input.service_count : 0; const podNames = (Array.isArray(input.pods_array) ? input.pods_array : []).slice(0, 10).map(p => p && p.name).filter(x => x); const depNames = (Array.isArray(input.deployments_array) ? input.deployments_array : []).slice(0, 10).map(d => d && d.name).filter(x => x); const svcNames = (Array.isArray(input.services_array) ? input.services_array : []).slice(0, 10).map(s => s && s.name).filter(x => x); return { pod_count: pc, deployment_count: dc, service_count: sc, sample_pod_names: podNames, sample_deployment_names: depNames, sample_service_names: svcNames }; })() }}

data.label: Resource Footprint Narrative
data.prompt:
You are an SRE writing a 3-4 bullet operator-facing characterization of namespace {{steps.analyze.namespace_label}}'s resource footprint.

Totals: {{steps.analyze.pod_count}} pods, {{steps.analyze.deployment_count}} deployments, {{steps.analyze.service_count}} services.

Resource sample (JSON):
{{steps.analyze.summary_payload}}

Output rules (MUST follow):
1. Start the response with a single `-` character — no preamble, no "Here is", no "The user wants", no thinking out loud.
2. 3-4 short bullets, each on its own line, each beginning with `-`.
3. Bullet 1: what kind of workload runs here (based on the resource names you see).
4. Bullet 2: any signs of risk worth calling out (concentrated components, unfamiliar services, single-replica deployments).
5. Bullet 3: network exposure observation (service types — ClusterIP vs LoadBalancer vs NodePort).
6. Bullet 4 (optional): a capacity/headroom note if relevant.
7. If the namespace is empty (pod_count, deployment_count, and service_count all 0), emit one bullet stating the namespace is empty and one bullet suggesting verification.
